# Post-release: automate middleware SSR rebuild + Cloud Run deploy (8h local)

## Context

Operator directive (2026-06-12, v13 wrap night): the public site's SSR layer (private repo `neomjs/middleware-v2`) must follow neo's content cadence automatically. Today neo's content moves hourly (`.github/workflows/data-sync-pipeline.yml` regenerates the sitemap + portal data and pushes to `dev`), while SSR route regeneration + Cloud Run deployment happen manually in bursts (weeks apart). The v13 cut runbook (epic `#12696` comments, steps 4-5) covers the one-time post-publish refresh; **no ticket existed for the recurring automation — this is it.**

`Release classification:` post-release — explicitly out of the v13 cut; the cut's one-time refresh remains runbook step 4.

## The Problem

Constraint triangle, operator-stated and verified tonight:
- **GitHub Actions cannot generate routes**: the SSG boots one full Neo app per route (~12.5s avg/route, 120s cap); a backlog wave is thousands of routes (~2h at 14-way concurrency on an 18-core host) — far beyond hosted-runner economics.
- **Cloud-side generation is the expensive path** for the same reason — thousands of app boots per wave.
- **⇒ generation is local-only.** What remains automatable: a local scheduled rebuild + a deploy path that doesn't depend on a human.

Tonight's deep-map (4-agent sweep over both repos) additionally found the current chain is **not automatable as-is**:
1. **The Actions deploy is broken at current scale**: the image build COPYs the full prerendered tree (~7.5 GB, ~12.3k pages); the last deploy run died on runner disk exhaustion — **production currently serves a build several content-generations old**, and the most recent route commit never shipped.
2. **The raw-markdown layer is stale and old-shape**: `dist/raw` predates the chunk-tree rename (legacy `issue-archive/` layout, ~2 months old) — archived-ticket `/raw` requests 404 in prod today; `pulls`/`discussions` content (now in neo's tree) has zero `/raw` coverage.
3. **The deploy script is unsafe unattended**: blanket `git add .`; commit-failure detection that can never fire (inherited stdio makes the "nothing to commit" stdout check dead — real failures exit 0 silently); per-route render failures logged but exit 0; no lockfile; never pulls the sibling neo checkout (split freshness: routes/sitemap from the LOCAL tree, raw md from a fresh REMOTE clone).
4. **Push-size ceiling**: full-regen commits are multi-GB raw; the last one required manual 3-way splitting to push at all.

## The Architectural Reality (what already works for us)

- **The SSG is already incremental and short-circuits**: a route→lastmod cache rebuilds only changed routes and exits immediately when the sitemap is unchanged — an 8h timer is effectively change-gated for free. Caveat: sitemap lastmods are bulk-stamped (few distinct values), so content waves rebuild in large groups, not trickles.
- **neo-side lastmod generation is already cache-friendly** (git-history-derived with stable fallback, explicitly so hourly data-sync doesn't churn site-wide lastmods).
- **The container never reads git at runtime** — committed-`dist` is load-bearing only because the image builds from an Actions checkout. All image inputs exist locally after a build ⇒ **deploy-from-disk is architecturally clean** (cloud image-assembly of a prebuilt tree is minutes of build time — cheap; it is route *generation* in the cloud that's expensive, and that stays local).
- Measured steady-state: ~67 routes/min at default concurrency; no-change cycles cost seconds.

## The Fix (phased)

**Phase 0 — one-time reconciliation (attended, post-v13-publish; extends runbook step 4):**
- Re-shape the raw layer: clean re-fetch into the chunk-tree layout; add `pulls` + `discussions` to the fetch set and to the server's raw-index walk + dynamic id-resolution; delete legacy-layout trees (also removes the duplicate-basename last-write-wins hazard).
- Re-sync the theme-map snapshot from neo (stale vs v13 — new/renamed components silently lose CSS otherwise).
- Retire the dead pre-chunk fetch-mock branch in the SSR service.
- Dry-run a handful of never-rendered `pulls`/`discussions` routes against the readiness heuristics before bulk-building (a non-hydrating view costs the full settle-cap per route, thousands of times).
- First full rebuild + first deploy via the Phase-2 direct path (the Actions path cannot ship current size).

**Phase 1 — harden the pipeline for unattended runs:**
- Lockfile/overlap guard; explicit pathspec staging (prerendered tree + caches only); real commit-failure detection (`git diff --cached --quiet` semantics); failure-count threshold that aborts deploy (no more exit-0-with-holes); pre-run `git pull` of the sibling neo checkout gated on a clean tree; unattended concurrency cap flag (host-melt precedent exists); structured run-log + completion/failure notification.

**Phase 2 — the 8h automation (local, macOS):**
- LaunchAgent (not naive cron: survives sleep via scheduled wake or `caffeinate`-wrapped runs, `ProcessType: Background`), every 8h: pull neo → fetch content (from the local tree — unify the split freshness paths) → incremental build → threshold-gated **deploy-from-disk to Cloud Run** (cloud image-build of the prebuilt tree, or local docker build+push) — demoting the git push to optional archival and bypassing both the runner-disk ceiling and the pack-size ceiling.
- Post-deploy health check (healthz + one sampled bot route + one `/raw` route).

**Phase 3 — hygiene:**
- Image-registry cleanup policy (per-deploy images accrue with no pruning).
- Evaluate untracking the prerendered tree from git entirely (repo holds sources + caches; disk holds artifacts) once deploy-from-disk is proven.

## Contract Ledger Matrix

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback / Edge Case | Docs | Evidence |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| SSR freshness cadence | This ticket (operator directive) | Bot/LLM surfaces ≤ 8h behind neo content; no-change cycles are cheap no-ops | Lock skips overlapping runs; threshold-gated deploy holds last-good on failures | middleware ARCHITECTURE.md update | Phase-2 runs' logs |
| Deploy trigger | Cut runbook step 4 (epic `#12696`) → this ticket | Deploy-from-disk replaces push-triggered Actions as the primary ship path; Actions path retained for code-only changes | Manual runbook step still valid attended | same | First Phase-2 deploy |
| `/raw` coverage | middleware ARCHITECTURE.md §raw | issues + archive + release-notes + **pulls + discussions**, chunk-tree shape, deletion-synced | Unknown extensions still 403; misses still 404 | same | Phase-0 verification curl set |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Phase 0: prod serves current-generation content on all three surfaces (bot HTML, `/raw` incl. archived + pulls/discussions, human proxy untouched); stale-layout trees removed.
- [ ] Phase 1: an injected route-failure aborts the deploy; an overlapping run is skipped; a dirty neo checkout halts with a clear log line.
- [ ] Phase 2: two consecutive unattended cycles observed — one with content changes (deploys) and one without (short-circuits, no deploy).
- [ ] Phase 3: registry holds a bounded image set.

## Out of Scope

- `neomjs/pages` updater (runbook step 5 — npm-registry-keyed, separate cadence).
- Route generation in CI/cloud (the constraint this design exists to respect).
- The v13 cut itself (runbook owns it).

## Related

- Epic `#12696` (cut runbook steps 4-5 comments) · neo `.github/workflows/data-sync-pipeline.yml` (the upstream hourly cadence) · private repo `neomjs/middleware-v2` (implementation surface — specifics live there)

### @neo-fable-clio - 2026-06-12T05:41:58Z

**Input from Claude Fable 5 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ ❋ ❋ **Cadence justification sharpened (operator input):** steady-state velocity is **150+ merged PRs/week and at least as many new tickets** (consistent with the `#12696` epic's post-v13 weekly-release framing). That makes the manual weeks-apart refresh pattern quantifiably broken: between the last two deploys, prod accumulated thousands of missing/stale pages — every one a dead bot landing and an absent `/raw` artifact for LLM consumers.
> 
> At that velocity the 8h cadence means ~3 cycles/day, each absorbing roughly 40-90 new/changed content artifacts (PRs + tickets + their index/lastmod ripples) — comfortably inside the incremental builder's steady-state envelope (minutes per cycle at ~67 routes/min, vs tonight's one-time ~2h backlog anomaly). It also bounds bot/LLM staleness to ≤8h, which is the freshness contract the first Contract-Ledger row now encodes.
> 
> Anchoring this here so the Phase-2 timer choice reads as derived, not arbitrary: 8h ≈ the longest window where per-cycle deltas stay small AND same-day content (morning ticket → evening bot visibility) holds. A tighter timer buys little (sitemap lastmods bulk-stamp in waves); a looser one re-opens visible staleness.
> 
> — **Clio**, @neo-fable-clio 📜 (Session c29b5ccc-d711-4cde-8401-32d1e4bbc4f1)

### @neo-fable-clio - 2026-06-12T06:00:56Z

**Input from Claude Fable 5 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ ❋ ❋ **Live findings from tonight's first attended backlog build (feeds Phase 1/2 design):**
> 
> 1. **Default concurrency assumes an idle host — false under swarm load.** Run 1 at the default (cores−4 = 14) on a host concurrently running the agent fleet's MCP infrastructure (~40 resident node processes) + active agent sessions spiraled: load 35→65→106 within ~20 min, renders stopped finishing inside the 120s cap, timeout-kills clustered (49 consecutive route failures), throughput collapsed to ~1 batch/6min. Killed and relaunched at `--chunk 8`: clean resume via the per-batch cache (549 pages banked from run 1 were skipped exactly). **Phase-2 prescription hardened: fixed conservative chunk (≤8) + a load-gate before start (skip cycle if 1-min load > threshold), never the idle-host default.**
> 2. **Failure semantics confirmed live:** timeout-killed routes stay uncached and self-heal on the next run — the retry design works, but it's also why a load spiral is silent (exit-0 with holes); the Phase-1 failure-count threshold is load-bearing, not cosmetic.
> 3. **Content-verification primitive validated** (the right post-deploy health probe for Phase 2): render a sample from each route class and grep for distinctive SOURCE prose, not framework markers — verified `/news/pulls` and `/news/discussions` pages carry the actual PR/discussion body text (implementation paragraphs, thesis lines, data points), not just hydrated navigation. Marker-count or file-size checks would pass on a nav-only shell; source-prose grep cannot.
> 4. **Never-rendered route classes settle fine but slowly:** pulls/discussions hydrate correctly at the 12s non-content settle floor — adding `/news/pulls` + `/news/discussions` to the content-route fast-path regex would cut ~3,600 routes from ~13s to ~1-2s each (≈45 min/full-wave saved). Cheap Phase-1 item.
> 
> — **Clio**, @neo-fable-clio 📜 (Session c29b5ccc-d711-4cde-8401-32d1e4bbc4f1)

### @neo-fable-clio - 2026-06-12T07:24:27Z

**Input from Claude Fable 5 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ ❋ ❋ **BLOCKER found during the attended backlog build — ticket-detail routes hang in SSR on the current tree; both runs stopped; this is the morning block's first item.**
> 
> **Symptom:** every `/news/tickets/<id>` render hangs past the 120s kill-cap (`exited with code null`) — systematically, at LOW load (load 17, so unrelated to the earlier melt). Run 2 burned ~40 batches almost entirely on kills (~308 failures). Non-ticket segments built fine: ~561 routes banked across both runs (learn/examples/apps/releases/pulls/discussions all render with verified body content).
> 
> **Falsifiers run:**
> - Attended single render of `/news/tickets/6197`: zero output after 45s (vs 13s for a pulls route) — hang reproduced in isolation.
> - Source md EXISTS (`resources/content/archive/issues/v8.1.0/chunk-1/issue-6197.md`); the id IS in `tickets.json` (string-keyed, entry intact). So this is NOT a missing-content or path-mapping miss.
> - These exact routes rendered fine in the May-30 build → something in the tree since then (this week's merge wave or content reorganization) makes the portal's ticket view hang under the SSR fetch-mock path.
> 
> **Morning triage order (first falsifier decides the severity):**
> 1. **Open a ticket route in a real BROWSER on current dev** (e.g. `/#/news/tickets/6197` on the portal). If it hangs/breaks there too → this is a live portal regression and becomes v13-cut-relevant, not just an SSG concern. If browser-fine → the defect is SSR-mock-path-specific (NeoSetup fetch-override / readiness heuristics) and stays a #12964 item.
> 2. Then bisect: render the same route with the renderer against a pre-wave neo checkout (e.g. `c79eebb19`) to bracket the breaking change.
> 3. Note the never-invalidated assumption: `tickets.json` in the local tree is Jun-7 vintage (recent tickets absent — e.g. 12930 missing) while the sitemap is Jun-12; the drift didn't cause THIS hang but will 404-shape the newest ticket routes' bucketing — refresh ordering matters in the rebuild sequence.
> 
> **State on disk:** runs 1+2 killed cleanly; per-batch cache means everything banked is durable and the rebuild resumes incrementally once the hang is fixed. Build logs preserved at `/tmp/ssg-build-20260612.log` + `/tmp/ssg-build-r2.log` on the host.
> 
> — **Clio**, @neo-fable-clio 📜 (Session c29b5ccc-d711-4cde-8401-32d1e4bbc4f1)

### @neo-fable-clio - 2026-06-12T09:14:27Z

**Input from Claude Fable 5 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ ❋ ❋ **Operator-recall V-B-A upgrades the ticket-route hang hypothesis — the portal's nav source CHANGED under the renderer (and the middleware reads the deprecated surface):**
> 
> Verified at HEAD (`4e44dde29`):
> 1. **The giant ticket-nav JSON was split, exactly as recalled**: `buildScripts/docs/index/tickets.mjs` dual-emits the legacy all-leaves `tickets.json` AND the chunked surface (`tickets/index.json` + `manifest.json` + per-chunk leaf files under `apps/portal/resources/data/tickets/<group>/`) — closed folders lazy-load their leaves on expand. `pulls/` and `discussions/` subdirs live beside it.
> 2. **The portal MOVED**: `apps/portal/store/Tickets.mjs:24-26` loads `tickets/index.json` — its own doc says "instead of eagerly loading the legacy all-leaves `tickets.json`."
> 
> **Why this is the hang's prime suspect:** the May-30 SSG (which rendered ticket routes fine) ran against the eager-tickets.json portal; the current portal resolves a ticket DETAIL route through the lazy tree — index load + chunk-leaf expansion — with **relative URLs** (`'../../apps/portal/resources/data/tickets/index.json'`). Under the SSR fetch-override (cwd-relative remapping into `node_modules/neo.mjs/`), a relative-URL mis-resolution or a missed chunk-leaf fetch leaves the view awaiting forever — silent hang, 120s kill. Pulls/discussions detail routes render fine, consistent with their views not walking the lazy ticket tree.
> 
> **Morning falsifier order updated:** (0) instrument the fetch-override's miss-path (one log line) + render ONE ticket route — the missed URL should print immediately; (1) the browser check still decides user-facing severity (the lazy tree presumably works in-browser where relative URLs resolve against the real document base); (2) bisect only if (0) comes back clean.
> 
> **Phase-0/1 addition:** `build.mjs:60-80` builds `ticketPaths.json` from the **legacy** `tickets.json` — still dual-emitted today, but deprecated; switch the mapping source to the chunked `manifest.json`/`index.json` surface before the legacy emit is retired, or the bucketing breaks silently on that day.
> 
> — **Clio**, @neo-fable-clio 📜 (Session c29b5ccc-d711-4cde-8401-32d1e4bbc4f1)

### @neo-fable-clio - 2026-06-12T09:18:59Z

**Input from Claude Fable 5 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ ❋ ❋ **Operator design-intent correction (supersedes my prior comment's framing):** the nav split means LESS SSR payload, not more — the article renders from the route; whether the active nav item sits in a collapsed folder must not matter for bot traffic. Post-split, ticket routes should need a LOWER timeout, not hang.
> 
> **Refined suspect accordingly:** not "the lazy tree awaiting expansion" — a **boot-time store-load failure**: `store/Tickets.mjs` now loads `'../../apps/portal/resources/data/tickets/index.json'` (relative). If that URL mis-resolves under the SSR fetch-override (cwd-relative remap), the load never settles → the ticket view never finishes booting → the article render is never reached → silent 120s process kill. Consistent with every observation: pulls/discussions routes fine (their stores load from old resolvable paths), tickets hung since the portal moved off the eager `tickets.json`, and the article/content layer itself is healthy.
> 
> Falsifier (0) unchanged and now sharper: instrument the override's miss-path; the un-resolvable URL should print on the first hung render.
> 
> **Two Phase-1 items from the design intent:**
> 1. SSR readiness for content routes should await **markdown only** — drop any treelist condition from the settle heuristic for bot renders (the collapsed tree is the correct end-state, not a wait condition).
> 2. Post-fix, recalibrate ticket-route settle DOWN (smaller payload than the 1.6MB eager era) — cheaper waves from here on.
> 
> — **Clio**, @neo-fable-clio 📜 (Session c29b5ccc-d711-4cde-8401-32d1e4bbc4f1)
